page: Microsoft Azure E2_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine configured with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server allocates dedicated CPU resources via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The physical layout consists of 1 physical core supporting 2 threads. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance asymmetry. Single-core and single-threaded operations, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and gzip compression, achieve average ratings. However, multi-core and memory-intensive benchmarks show significant limitations. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and memory bandwidth are rated as poor or weak. Similarly, database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving capacities fall into the lower performance tiers.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E2_v4 offers a dense memory footprint relative to its low vCPU count, making it a viable option for workloads that require substantial memory capacity but do not demand high parallel processing power. It is best suited for small-scale databases, memory-cached applications, lightweight enterprise services, and basic web serving where memory capacity is the primary bottleneck rather than raw compute throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure E2_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, delivering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz, this server utilizes the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation. It contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core and single-threaded capabilities, but multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations are relatively weak. Qualitatively, this server provides a cost-efficient entry point for memory-heavy workloads that do not require intensive parallel computing. It is best suited for small databases, caching layers, and lightweight enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, optimized for memory-intensive workloads with low compute demands.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2_v4 is an Ev4-family VM with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-heavy, low-compute tasks.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight, memory-intensive enterprise workloads and small databases.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core
Dedicated CPU allocation via Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor
No bundled local storage or GPU acceleration
Best suited for small databases and caching applications
